methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $26M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

private.name: Fuse Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Deep Isolation, a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Elizabeth Muller, has raised $26M to date. The valuation universe for nuclear energy comprises 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Fuse Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Oklo. A narrow peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the nuclear energy sector—only one same-stage peer existed, falling short of the three-datapoint minimum required for statistical reliability.

Stage-progression methodology, which applies a 2.0x multiplier to total capital raised as a Series A-to-next-stage step-up, represents the sole applicable triangulation method. Using the $26M raised as a valuation proxy and applying the standard step-up multiplier yields an implied valuation of $52M.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $44M to $60M, with a median point of $52M. The range reflects typical variance around the stage-progression output but is anchored to a single method. Key uncertainties include the absence of tight nuclear energy peer multiples at Deep Isolation's stage and the reliance on a historical step-up multiplier that may not fully capture sector-specific investor expectations or the company's capital intensity. Additional revenue or product-stage data would improve confidence in this estimate.
